task_completion.description: Location is set to Royal Observatory Greenwich.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Stellarium's location window shows 'Royal Observatory Greenwich' or coordinates near 51.4769 N, 0.0005 W, or the bottom status bar indicates this location.

task_completion.description: Display is configured for a pedagogical view without obstructions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Stellarium sky view shows a black background (atmosphere disabled), no ground/landscape visible at the bottom, visible constellation lines connecting stars, and the ecliptic line drawn across the sky. Planet labels should be visible.

task_completion.description: Mars is selected and centered in the view.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Mars is visible in the sky, selected (usually indicated by a reticle or crosshair), and positioned in the center of the screen.

task_completion.description: The date and time are set to the required observation moments.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Date/Time window or bottom status bar shows the time as 22:00:00 and the date as one of the required dates: 2022-10-30, 2022-12-08, or 2023-01-12.

task_completion.description: Screenshots of the configured views are captured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An on-screen notification in Stellarium indicating a screenshot was saved, or a file manager window showing image files in /home/ga/Pictures/stellarium/.

task_completion.description: A teaching worksheet answer key is created with the required content.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'mars_retrograde_notes.txt' (ideally saved on the Desktop). The text contains the three dates (Oct 30 2022, Dec 8 2022, Jan 12 2023), the words 'retrograde' and 'opposition', and a brief explanation mentioning the year 2022 or 2023.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Stellarium GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Stellarium application interface being actively used, rather than just terminal commands editing configuration files.

integrity.description: Screenshots were generated from the application, not downloaded.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots of the sky view match the Stellarium interface and the specific configurations requested, rather than looking like stock images from a web browser.

integrity.description: The notes file contains a coherent explanation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text in the notes file reads like a brief explanation or worksheet answer key, rather than a direct copy-paste of the task description prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires configuring Stellarium to the Royal Observatory Greenwich (51.4769° N, 0.0005° W). Display settings must have atmosphere and landscape disabled, while the ecliptic line and constellation lines are enabled. Mars must be centered. Three screenshots are required at exactly 22:00 UTC on 2022-10-30, 2022-12-08, and 2023-01-12. The notes file must be saved at /home/ga/Desktop/mars_retrograde_notes.txt and contain the three dates, the terms 'retrograde' and 'opposition', and mention 2022 or 2023.
